name: Filled Berlin Doughnuts (Bismarks)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the fat to 375 degrees F. for frying the doughnuts.
2. Soften the yeast in the warm water and let stand for 5 minutes or until it "blooms" or proofs.
3. In a large bowl, mix together sugar, salt, butter, orange juice, and rum extract.
4. Pour in the scalded milk and stir until the butter is melted. Cool to lukewarm.
5. Blend in 1 cup of unbleached flour and beat until smooth.
6. Stir in the yeast and add about half of the remaining flour, beating until smooth.
7. Beat in the eggs. Then beat in enough of the remaining flour to make a soft (should be slightly sticky and light in weight) dough.
8. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured board and let rest for 5 to 10 minutes.
9. Knead until smooth and elastic (about 8 to 10 minutes).
10. Form into a ball and put into a greased bowl, turning to grease the top of the dough.
11. Cover and let rise in a warm draft-free place until doubled in bulk.
12. Punch down the dough, kneading lightly to remove all air pockets, and turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
13. Roll to 1/2-inch thickness.
14. Cut the dough into rounds using a 3-inch biscuit or cookie cutter.
15. Cover with waxed paper and let rise on the rolling surface away from drafts and direct heat until doubled in bulk (30 to 45 minutes).
16. About 20 minutes before frying, heat the fat.
17. Fry the doughnuts in the heated fat, adding only as many at a time as will float uncrowded in a single layer deep in the fat.
18. Fry 2 to 3 minutes or until lightly browned; turn with a fork or tongs when they rise to the surface.
19. Lift from the fat, draining for a few seconds over the fat before removing to absorbent paper toweling.
20. Cut a slit through the center of each doughnut.
21. Force about 1/2 teaspoon of jam or jelly into the center and press lightly to close the slit.
22. Shake 2 or 3 bismarks at a time in a bag containing granulated or powdered sugar.
